original_abstract: Education serves as a vital pillar in ensuring the continuity and progress of a nation. However, increasing instances of juvenile delinquency—including physical altercations, drug abuse, and other moral lapses—signal a troubling trend of moral degradation. To combat this, schools must prioritize character building and personality development rooted in religious values. This study explores the "refraction method" (habituation and modeling) as a critical tool for instilling positive traits in children. When the school environment and social surroundings align with Islamic educational teachings, students’ character values flourish. Specifically, this research investigates the implementation of the refraction method at SDN Babakan Sirna Sukabumi. The study addresses four key areas: (1) the primary objectives of using the refraction method for character building; (2) the specific forms and practices of refraction utilized within the school; (3) the evaluation systems in place and the resulting character traits observed in students; and (4) the facilitating factors and obstacles encountered in fostering these Islamic values.
humanized_abstract: Education is one of the main components in order to maintain and develop the nation. In recent years, there has been an increase in juvenile crime committed by students. Many students have engaged in physical fighting, drug abuse, and other acts of deviant behavior. For this reason, it is necessary for schools to develop a curriculum that promotes the student’s character and personality based on religious values. This study used the refraction method (habituation and modeling) in an attempt to develop the students’ character. It is believed that if the school and the surrounding community that support the Islamic education of students, then the students’ character will be developed in line with the desired Islamic character. This study aims to examine the refraction method in developing students’ character in SDN Babakan Sirna Sukabumi. The study covered four aspects of the implementation of refraction in developing students’ character, namely: 1) The purpose of developing students’ character by using refraction method; 2) The forms and practices of refraction used in schools; 3) The assessment of refraction in developing students’ character and the character of students as a result of that; and 4) The factors that support and hamper the development of Islamic character.
